meaning: n.pl.危险,危险期,紧要关头(crisis的复数形式)
examples: We encounter emergencies and personal crises.
Mr. Varin is new to the car industry, which is itself nearly a year into one of the most brutal crises in its history.
If the sky, and the share price is falling, outside directors should be able to give advice based on having weathered their own crises.
Few later-life crises have been as lucrative as the one that blessed Charles (Ted) Bauer in 1976.
Hopefully, it will lift the spirits of many Americans who are affected by the housing and economic crises.
